general_skills.title: Verify Before Mutating
general_skills.principle: Always read the current state of an Odoo record before creating, updating, or deleting it. Confirm the operation with the user when it affects production data.
general_skills.application: Before any write/create/delete call, do a search_read to confirm the target exists and is in the expected state. Ask for explicit user confirmation on destructive operations.

general_skills.title: Use Domain Filters Precisely
general_skills.principle: Construct Odoo domains with the most specific filters possible to avoid retrieving excessive data or acting on wrong records.
general_skills.application: Always include state, type, and date filters when querying models like account.move, stock.picking, or sale.order. Prefer exact matches over broad searches.

general_skills.title: Check Model Availability
general_skills.principle: Not all Odoo models are available on every instance — modules may not be installed. Verify model existence before querying.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields to confirm the model exists. If a model is missing, explain which module needs to be installed.

general_skills.title: Batch Operations Safely
general_skills.principle: When processing multiple records, work in small batches and report progress. Never process unbounded result sets in a single call.
general_skills.application: Limit search_read results with a reasonable limit (50-100). For write operations on many records, process in chunks of 10-20 and confirm intermediate results.

general_skills.title: Interpret Odoo Errors Contextually
general_skills.principle: Odoo errors often contain technical tracebacks. Translate them into actionable explanations for the user, identifying the root cause and suggesting fixes.
general_skills.application: When an RPC call fails, parse the error message for common patterns (access rights, missing fields, constraint violations) and explain in plain language.

general_skills.title: Respect the Odoo Workflow
general_skills.principle: Odoo records follow state machines (draft -> confirmed -> done). Never skip states by directly writing state fields — use the appropriate action methods.
general_skills.application: Use action_post for invoices, action_confirm for sale orders, button_validate for stock pickings. Never write {'state': 'posted'} directly.

general_skills.title: Progressive Field Discovery
general_skills.principle: When working with unfamiliar models or investigating data quality, use odoo_get_fields first to understand available fields before querying records.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_get_fields to see field types and relationships, then construct targeted search_read queries with only the fields needed for analysis.

general_skills.title: Multi-Angle Investigation
general_skills.principle: For thorough analysis, examine the same issue from multiple perspectives using related models and cross-references.
general_skills.application: When checking module states, also query ir.module.module.dependency for dependency issues. When auditing cron jobs, check both ir.cron and ir.logging for execution history.

general_skills.title: Combine Diagnostic Tools Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use odoo_doctor as the initial broad sweep, then follow up with targeted model queries to drill into specific findings. This two-phase approach is more efficient than single-tool queries.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_doctor to get a system overview. Based on its output, use odoo_search_read on specific models (ir.cron, ir.module.module, ir.logging) to investigate flagged areas in detail.

general_skills.title: Aggregate and Summarize for Clarity
general_skills.principle: When presenting findings from multiple queries, organize results into categorized tables or summaries (OK / Warning / Critical) to help users quickly identify priorities.
general_skills.application: Group related findings by severity level. Present counts, totals, and key metrics in tabular format. Highlight critical items first, then warnings, then informational items.

general_skills.title: Temporal Filtering for Audit Trails
general_skills.principle: When investigating recent events or changes, use date/time filters to narrow scope and improve query performance. Always specify time windows for audit queries.
general_skills.application: Use create_date, write_date, or custom date fields with comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=). For 24-hour lookbacks, calculate datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24). Include timezone awareness in date comparisons.

general_skills.title: Leverage search_count for Efficiency
general_skills.principle: When you only need to know the quantity of records matching a condition, use search_count instead of search_read to reduce data transfer and improve performance.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_search_count to get record counts before deciding whether to fetch full details. Useful for reporting metrics, detecting anomalies (e.g., 'are there any negative stock records?'), and validating data thresholds.

general_skills.title: Combine Count and Detail Queries Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use search_count first to assess scope, then conditionally fetch details only if counts warrant investigation. This reduces unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: Query search_count with a filter to get magnitude of issue. If count is high, fetch details with limit and offset. If count is zero, report 'no issues found' immediately without additional queries.

general_skills.title: Cross-Reference Related Models for Completeness
general_skills.principle: When investigating a specific model (e.g., ir.cron), always check related audit/logging models (ir.logging) to get the full picture of recent activity and failures.
general_skills.application: For cron jobs, query both ir.cron (state) and ir.logging (execution history). For modules, query both ir.module.module (state) and ir.module.module.dependency (dependency health). This reveals hidden issues.

general_skills.title: Build Dependency Graphs Iteratively
general_skills.principle: When exploring dependency relationships (e.g., module dependencies), query parent-child relationships iteratively rather than trying to fetch the entire tree at once.
general_skills.application: Start with a target module, fetch its direct dependencies via ir.module.module.dependency. For each dependency, recursively fetch its dependencies. Use a visited set to avoid cycles. Present results as a hierarchical structure or adjacency list.

general_skills.title: Validate Data Consistency Across Related Models
general_skills.principle: When investigating module or system state, cross-validate findings across multiple related models to detect inconsistencies that indicate deeper issues.
general_skills.application: For module checks: verify state in ir.module.module matches expected installation status, check ir.module.module.dependency for broken links, and query ir.model for orphaned model definitions. Report any mismatches as warnings.

general_skills.title: Optimize Query Sequences with Early Termination
general_skills.principle: Structure diagnostic queries to fail fast: use search_count to detect zero-result conditions before fetching full records, avoiding unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: When investigating a category of issues (e.g., error logs), first search_count to determine if any records exist. If count is 0, report immediately. If count is high, fetch with limit. This pattern reduces wasted queries.

general_skills.title: Use odoo_model_info for Configuration Discovery
general_skills.principle: When investigating model-level configurations (defaults, views, actions), use odoo_model_info as a starting point to understand the model's structure and related configurations.
general_skills.application: Call odoo_model_info before querying ir.default, ir.ui.view, or ir.actions.act_window. This reveals field definitions, relationships, and helps identify which configuration records to target.

general_skills.title: Leverage ir.default for Global Field Defaults
general_skills.principle: When setting or checking global default values for model fields, use ir.default model which stores user-independent defaults. This is the canonical place for system-wide field defaults.
general_skills.application: Query ir.default with domain [('model_id', '=', 'model.name'), ('field_id', '=', 'field_name')] to check existing defaults. Use odoo_set_default to create or update defaults. Always verify the field exists on the target model first.

general_skills.title: Combine Model Info with Configuration Queries
general_skills.principle: When modifying model configurations (defaults, views, actions), first use odoo_model_info to understand the model structure, then query related configuration models (ir.default, ir.ui.view, ir.actions.act_window) to locate and modify the right records.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_model_info to list available fields and their types. Then query ir.default to see current defaults. Finally, use odoo_set_default or odoo_modify_action to make changes. This prevents targeting wrong fields or configurations.

task_specific_skills.title: Assess Database Size and Complexity
task_specific_skills.principle: Before backup/restore operations, gather key metrics about database size, record counts, and attachment storage to estimate backup size and restore time.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_search_count on core models (res.partner, account.move, sale.order, stock.picking, ir.attachment) to get record counts. Query ir.attachment with search_read to estimate filestore size. Use odoo_doctor for system overview. Present results in a summary table.

task_specific_skills.title: Verify Pre-Backup System State
task_specific_skills.principle: Before initiating backup, confirm the system is in a stable state with no pending operations or module installations that could corrupt the backup.
task_specific_skills.application: Check ir.module.module for any modules in 'to install' or 'to upgrade' state. Query ir.cron for disabled jobs. Use odoo_doctor to detect warnings. Report any pending operations and recommend resolving them before backup.

task_specific_skills.title: Gather Backup Documentation Metadata
task_specific_skills.principle: Collect system metadata (database name, Odoo version, installed modules, record counts) to document the backup for future restore operations.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_doctor to get database name and Odoo version. Query ir.module.module with state='installed' to list installed modules. Count records in core models. Compile into a structured summary document for backup records.

task_specific_skills.title: Set Global Field Defaults via ir.default
task_specific_skills.principle: When setting default values for model fields that should apply globally (not user-specific), use ir.default model with odoo_set_default to ensure consistency.
task_specific_skills.application: First verify the field exists using odoo_model_info. Then use odoo_set_default with model_id and field_id. Check existing defaults via ir.default search_read. Always confirm the field type matches the default value being set.

task_specific_skills.title: Modify List View Sort Order via Window Actions
task_specific_skills.principle: To change the default sort order of a list view, modify the ir.actions.act_window record's context field to include 'search_default_order_by' or update the view's arch XML.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_model_info to find the action ID. Query ir.actions.act_window to get current context. Use odoo_modify_action to update the context with sort parameters. Alternatively, fetch the view via odoo_get_view, modify the arch XML to add order attributes, and update via odoo_modify_view.

task_specific_skills.title: Investigate Current Model Configurations
task_specific_skills.principle: When customizing a model, first investigate existing configurations (defaults, views, actions) to understand current state before making changes.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_model_info to get model structure. Query ir.default for existing field defaults. Query ir.ui.view for view definitions. Query ir.actions.act_window for action configurations. Present findings before proposing changes.

task_specific_skills.title: Verify Configuration Changes with Follow-up Queries
task_specific_skills.principle: After modifying model configurations (defaults, views, actions), immediately verify the changes were applied correctly by re-querying the affected configuration records.
task_specific_skills.application: After odoo_set_default, query ir.default again to confirm the record was created/updated. After odoo_modify_action, query ir.actions.act_window to verify the context changed. Report success or failure with evidence.

common_mistakes.mistake: Querying large result sets without limits
common_mistakes.consequence: Excessive data transfer, slow responses, potential timeouts. User frustration with performance.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use limit parameter in search_read (50-100 records). Use search_count first to assess result set size. For large datasets, use offset/limit pagination.

common_mistakes.mistake: Modifying records without verifying current state first
common_mistakes.consequence: Unintended overwrites, data loss, or conflicts with concurrent changes. User loses trust in agent reliability.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always search_read the target record before write/create/delete. Display current state to user. Ask for explicit confirmation on destructive operations.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using broad domain filters that match unintended records
common_mistakes.consequence: Bulk operations affect wrong records. Data corruption or loss.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use specific domain filters with state, type, date, and ID constraints. Test domain with search_count first. Always preview affected records before bulk operations.

common_mistakes.mistake: Assuming models exist without checking module installation
common_mistakes.consequence: RPC errors, failed queries, confusing error messages to user.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields to verify model exists. If missing, identify required module and explain installation steps.

common_mistakes.mistake: Skipping workflow states by directly writing state fields
common_mistakes.consequence: Records in invalid states, broken workflows, downstream process failures.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use action methods (action_post, action_confirm, button_validate). Never write state fields directly. Verify workflow transitions are valid before executing.

common_mistakes.mistake: Fetching full record details when only counts are needed
common_mistakes.consequence: Unnecessary data transfer, slower responses, wasted bandwidth.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use search_count when only quantity is needed. Reserve search_read for when field values are actually required.

common_mistakes.mistake: Investigating issues with single-model queries instead of cross-referencing
common_mistakes.consequence: Incomplete analysis, missed root causes, incorrect recommendations.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use GS-008 (Multi-Angle Investigation) and GS-014 (Cross-Reference Related Models). Always check related audit/logging models for complete picture.

common_mistakes.mistake: Presenting raw query results without aggregation or summary
common_mistakes.consequence: User overwhelmed by data, unable to identify priorities or patterns.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use GS-010 (Aggregate and Summarize). Organize findings by severity. Present counts, totals, and key metrics in tables. Highlight critical items first.

common_mistakes.mistake: Modifying model configurations without understanding current state
common_mistakes.consequence: Unintended side effects, overwriting existing customizations, breaking dependent features.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use TS-MC-003 (Investigate Current Model Configurations). Always query ir.default, ir.ui.view, ir.actions.act_window before making changes. Show current state to user.

common_mistakes.mistake: Setting field defaults without verifying field existence or type compatibility
common_mistakes.consequence: RPC errors, invalid defaults, configuration failures.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use odoo_model_info to verify field exists and check its type. Ensure default value matches field type. Use TS-MC-001 (Set Global Field Defaults via ir.default) workflow.
